macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Cuts Losses Amid Low Volume Trading; Tesla Drags Consumer Discretionary Lower
items.summary: U.S. stocks pared losses on reports related to the Strait of Hormuz, with the S&P 500 down 16.5% in volume; consumer staples gained while Tesla-led consumer discretionary, healthcare, and industrials lagged.[3]

items.title: SBA Communications Surges 19% on Potential Sale Exploration
items.summary: SBA Communications led S&P 500 gainers with a 19% rise after receiving preliminary takeover interest and exploring a potential sale.[3]

items.title: Amazon in Talks to Acquire Satellite Provider, Stock Up 13%
items.summary: Amazon is reportedly in discussions to buy a satellite provider to bolster its satellite operations, sending the stock up 13%.[3]

items.title: Nasdaq Enters Correction Territory Amid Iran War Supply Disruptions
items.summary: Stock markets, led by Nasdaq, have fallen about 10% from highs due to Iran war disruptions in Strait of Hormuz affecting energy, AI investments, semiconductors, and global supply chains.[4]

items.title: Gold Prices Drop Sharply as Oil Surges on Trump Comments
items.summary: Gold fell from two-week highs while oil prices rose after President Trump vowed continued action amid Iran tensions.[6]

items.title: Iran War Pressures Oil Prices and Global Supply Chains; OECD Cuts 2026 Growth to 2.9%
items.summary: Ongoing Iran war disrupts Strait of Hormuz, spiking oil and causing shortages in helium, aluminum; threatens AI boom and prompts OECD to lower global growth forecast to 2.9%.[1][4]
items.source: First Trust Economics Blog / Atlantic Council

items.title: Slower Real Wage Growth Expected Amid Energy Shock and Inflation
items.summary: March jobs report to show 3.7% annual wage growth, but inflation from Iran war oil shock may lead to declining real wages, hitting household spending.[2]

items.title: Putin Warns of Global Trade Shifts from Iran Conflict Disrupting Hormuz
items.summary: Russian President Putin highlights escalating U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict disrupting energy flows through Strait of Hormuz, reshaping global trade priorities.[5]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Sees $349M in Liquidations Amid Bitcoin and Ethereum Declines
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced $349.84 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, with Ethereum leading at $90.83 million and Bitcoin at $80.89 million, mostly from long positions. Bitcoin dropped 3.89% to around $66,200, influenced by President Trump's address on Iran conflict and rising oil prices.

items.title: Top Crypto Gainers: NTRN Surges 134.78%, STO Up 62.45% in Volatile 24 Hours
items.summary: Neutron (NTRN) led gainers with a 134.78% surge to $0.0054 on $5.12M volume, followed by Onomy Protocol (NOM) at 72.38% to $0.0101 and Stobox Token (STO) at 62.45% to $0.7286 with $1.63B volume. Other notable gainers include SOLV and MATH amid broader market volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin World Reports STO as Top Gainer with 103.29% Rally, NTRN Follows at 69.57%
items.summary: Storage Token (STO) topped gainers with 103.29% increase to $0.9118 on $1.18B volume, Neutron (NTRN) rose 69.57% to $0.0039, and Onomy Protocol (NOM) gained 32.70% to $0.0078. Market showed intense trading activity and shifting sentiment.

items.title: Goldman Sachs Predicts Further Bitcoin Decline Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Goldman Sachs maintains a bearish outlook on Bitcoin, which dropped 2.4% to $66,852.50, range-bound between $60K-$70K over the past month amid U.S.-Iran conflict. Bitcoin has fallen 46.9% from its $126K all-time high in October 2025.

items.title: $443M Crypto Liquidations with Bitcoin Longs Hit Hardest at $78.51M
items.summary: Total liquidations reached $443 million in 24 hours, with Bitcoin long positions liquidated for $78.51 million and Ethereum longs at $71.92 million. Short positions saw smaller losses, reflecting bearish sentiment.

items.title: Largest Crypto Liquidation: $17.17M Brent Oil Position on Hyperliquid
items.summary: The biggest single liquidation in the past 24 hours was a $17.17 million Brent oil position on Hyperliquid, surpassing Bitcoin and Ethereum liquidations amid heightened market volatility.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: MIT Develops SEED-SET: New AI Testing Method Flags Fairness Risks in Autonomous Systems
items.summary: Researchers at MIT have introduced SEED-SET, an AI testing method that generates scenarios to uncover fairness risks in autonomous systems like power grids and traffic routing, outperforming baseline strategies by generating over twice as many optimal test cases.

items.title: Sakana AI's 'The AI Scientist' Generates and Submits Peer-Reviewed Research Paper
items.summary: Tokyo-based Sakana AI has created 'The AI Scientist,' an autonomous system that independently conceived, wrote, and submitted a scientific paper accepted at a 2025 ICLR workshop, raising ethical questions about AI in research.

items.title: NIH Awards $30.7M to Expand USC-Led AI4AD Initiative for Alzheimer's Research
items.summary: The NIH has invested $30.7 million, including a new $12.5 million grant, to advance the AI4AD project led by USC, aiming to decode Alzheimer's disease through AI.
items.source: Keck School of Medicine of USC

items.title: Google DeepMind Researcher Discusses AI Automating Its Own R&D in Recent Podcast
items.summary: In a MAD Podcast episode, Google DeepMind's Mostafa Dehghani explores AI 'thinking in loops,' recursive self-improvement, and challenges like continual learning toward AGI.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Escape from Tarkov Reveals Prestige Stats and Unity 6 Integration
items.summary: Escape from Tarkov announced new Prestige stats from Nikita, Blicky buffs, a brand new quest, April Fools wipe surprise, in-game survey rewards, and potential Unity 6 engine upgrade.

items.title: Gray Zone Warfare Patch 0.4 Released with Major Improvements
items.summary: Gray Zone Warfare's Patch 0.4 update is now live, featuring significant enhancements after 40 hours of testing; developers acknowledge ongoing issues like LZ camping but focus on core vision.

items.title: Valorant Pro Miks' Debut in VCT Raises Questions on Future
items.summary: New Valorant pro Miks played his first VCT games with DRG against XLG in China, prompting analysis on whether these were his last amid team decisions.

items.title: The Last of Us Online Reportedly 80% Complete Before Cancellation
items.summary: Kinda Funny Games Daily discusses rumors that The Last of Us Online multiplayer mode was 80% complete prior to its cancellation.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Artemis II Crew Prepares for Historic Moon Mission Launch
items.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is set to launch on April 1, 2026, with a crew preparing for a 10-day, 600,000-mile journey to the moon. The mission follows in the footsteps of Apollo 8 with similar objectives for lunar exploration.

items.title: YouTube and Meta Ordered to Pay $6 Million in Landmark Damages Trial
items.summary: A jury has decided that YouTube and Meta should pay $6 million in damages in a significant legal case, marking a landmark ruling in the tech industry.

items.title: NASA Announces $20 Billion Accelerated Plan to Build Moon Base
items.summary: NASA has unveiled an accelerated $20 billion initiative to construct a moon base as part of its long-term lunar exploration strategy.
